items.title: White House posts then deletes Easter video of Trump insulting Macron
items.description: The White House briefly published — then deleted — a video from Donald Trump’s private Easter luncheon in which the US president made a series of inflammatory remarks, and repeated false claims about several public figures. These extraordinarily candid comments were likely never supposed to be heard by the public. However, the closed-door footage was downloaded by a journalist before it was removed and later circulated online.

items.title: Donald Trump has repeatedly delayed deadlines for Iran, but suggests Tuesday’s is final
items.description: Trump said he’s “not at all” concerned about committing war crimes with his planned attacks on Iran

items.title: News24 | Trump taunts Iran with ‘Praise be to Allah’ after airman rescue, demands Hormuz be opened
items.description: US President Donald Trump and other US officials cast the rescue of a US airman in Iran as an “Easter miracle”, framing the operation in religious terms that portrayed the war as a just cause and divinely ‌blessed.

items.title: Cairo goes dark as Egypt struggles to cope with fall out from Iran war
items.description: Normally bustling with activity until the early hours of the morning, the streets of Cairo have gone dark. Soaring fuel costs have pushed the Egyptian government to issue a month-long early-closing order. Now shops are shuttered at 9pm during the week and 10pm on weekends.

items.title: News24 | US fighter jet shot down over Iran, search underway for crew
items.description: A US fighter jet was shot down over ‌Iran and a ⁠search and rescue operation ⁠was underway for any survivors, a US official said on Friday.

items.title: India sends emergency relief supplies to flood and earthquake-hit Afghanistan
items.description: External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said India is committed to extending humanitarian assistance to the Afghan people in this challenging time

items.title: Displaced in Lebanon: 'Lives turned upside down'
items.description: Over 1.1 million Lebanese have fled their homes due to fighting in the south of the country and Israeli bombing of Beirut's suburbs. DW met a family struggling to preserve a sense of normality in tough conditions.

items.title: Trump steps up Iran threats, highlights successful rescue mission
items.description: US President Donald Trump said Monday he would lay waste to every bridge and power plant in Iran if it fails to bend to his demands, as he touted the high-risk operation that rescued two downed airmen.

items.title: Iran threatens to restrict Bab el-Mandeb Strait after Trump warnings
items.description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened Iran and its infrastructure if it doesn't open the Strait of Hormuz by his Tuesday deadline. In response, Iran threatened to restrict the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.

items.title: Ukraine’s Zhytomyr region reels after missile and drone strike
items.description: Russian missile and drone strikes hit Ukraine’s Zhytomyr region, killing one and injuring 10, as dozens of homes and public buildings were destroyed.

items.title: Bandits kill police officer, two others in Katsina
items.description: The officer was killed when the bandits attacked a local police station in the state. The post Bandits kill police officer, two others in Katsina appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Ukraine: 3 killed in Odesa, Kyiv targets Russian oil exports
items.description: Russian drone strikes on the southern Ukrainian city of Odesa left at least three people dead overnight, including a 2-year-old girl. Meanwhile, Ukraine has continued retaliatory strikes against Russian oil facilities.

items.title: Germany's new religious diversity
items.description: In Germany, many Christian churches are abandoned. In their place, other religious buildings are emerging. Immigrants from India or Syria bring their matters of faith, making the urban landscape increasingly diverse.

items.title: Local culture shapes the Zairean liturgy
items.description: Easter in the Democratic Republic of the Congo—where congregants celebrate with the Zairean Rite, the only Vatican-approved liturgy shaped by local culture, alive with song, dance, and striking ritual.

items.title: German Chancellor Merz has never been more unpopular
items.description: The CDU/CSU and SPD made many promises to voters. But according to polls, Germans are deeply disappointed with the governing coalition and have little hope that things will improve.

items.title: German men need military permit for extended stays abroad
items.description: The new military service law requires all men under 45 to seek approval from the Bundeswehr to leave the country for longer than three months. It also obliges the military career center to issue it.

items.description: The crew will not land on the Moon on this current mission, though Nasa is preparing for a potential lunar landing by 2028.
